# tsb_traffic_service_route (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Short name of the ServiceRoute resource.
- `parent` (String) The parent id for the ServiceRoute resource.
- `service` (String) The service on which the configuration is being applied. Must be in namespace/FQDN format.

### Optional

- `argo_rollout` (Attributes) ArgoRollout configures this ServiceRoute to be controlled by Argo Rollouts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--argo_rollout))
- `config_generation_metadata` (Attributes) Metadata values that will be add into the Istio generated configurations. When using YAML APIs like `tctl` or `gitops`, put them into the `metadata.labels` or `metadata.annotations` instead. This field is only necessary when using gRPC APIs directly. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config_generation_metadata))
- `description` (String) A description of the resource.
- `display_name` (String) User friendly name for the resource.
- `http_routes` (Attributes List) HTTPRoutes are used when HTTP traffic needs to be matched on uri, headers and port and destination routes need to be set using subset-weight combinations specified within the route. **Note**: If a route is specified, then the global subset-weight combinations (specified under subsets) will be ignored for the matched port, as subsets within route will take effect. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes))
- `port_level_settings` (Attributes List) In order to support multi-protocol routing, a list of all port/protocol combinations is needed. These port settings are applied to all the subsets (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--port_level_settings))
- `sticky_session` (Attributes) StickySession specifies how to forward traffic from a client to the same backend (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sticky_session))
- `subsets` (Attributes List) The set of versions of a service and the percentage of traffic to send to each version. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsets))
- `tcp_routes` (Attributes List) TCPRoutes match TCP traffic based on port number. The subset-weight configuration and priority have the same behaviour as HTTPRoutes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tcp_routes))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Fully-qualified name of the ServiceRoute resource.

<a id="nestedatt--argo_rollout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `argo_rollout`

Optional:

- `destination_rule_name` (String) The name of the DestinationRule that the ServiceRoute will create that should be configured in the Argo Rollout custom resource.
- `virtual_service_name` (String) The name of the VirtualService that the ServiceRoute will create that should be configured in the Argo Rollout custom resource.


<a id="nestedatt--config_generation_metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config_generation_metadata`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.annotations` field of the Istio generated configurations.
- `labels` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.labels` field of the Istio generated configurations.


<a id="nestedatt--http_routes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the route.

Optional:

- `destination` (Attributes List) Destination host:port and subset where HTTP traffic should be directed. **Note**: Only one of `destination` and `flagger` must be configured per route. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--destination))
- `fault` (Attributes) Fault injection policy to apply on HTTP traffic at the client side. Note that timeouts or retries will not be enabled when faults are enabled on the client side. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--fault))
- `flagger` (Attributes) FlaggerDestination will route traffic based on a Flagger Canary resource. **Note**: Only one of `destination` and `flagger` must be configured per route. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--flagger))
- `match` (Attributes List) Match conditions for incoming HTTP traffic (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--match))
- `mirrors` (Attributes List) Mirror HTTP traffic to multiple destinations in addition to forwarding the requests to the intended destination. Mirrored traffic is on a best effort basis, so it won't wait for the mirrored destinations response to respond to the intended destination. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--mirrors))

<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.destination`

Required:

- `port` (Number) The port corresponding to the service host where traffic should be routed

Optional:

- `destination_host` (String) Service host where traffic should be routed to. This should either be a FQDN or a short name for the k8s service. For example, "reviews" as destination_host will be interpreted as "reviews.ns1.cluster.local" If empty, the host will be inferred from the Service Route service field.
- `subset` (String) Subset is the version of the service where traffic should be routed to
- `weight` (Number) Weight defines the amount of traffic that needs to be routed to this specific version


<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--fault"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.fault`

Optional:

- `abort` (Attributes) Abort HTTP request attempts and return error codes back to downstream service, giving the impression that the upstream service is faulty. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--fault--abort))
- `delay` (Attributes) Delay requests before forwarding, emulating various failures such as network issues, overloaded upstream service, etc. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--fault--delay))

<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--fault--abort"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.fault.abort`

Optional:

- `grpc_status` (String) GRPC status code to use to abort the request. The supported codes are documented in https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/statuscodes.md
- `http_status` (Number) HTTP status code to use to abort the HTTP request.
- `percentage` (Number) Percentage of requests to be aborted with the error code provided. If not specified, no request will be aborted.


<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--fault--delay"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.fault.delay`

Optional:

- `fixed_delay` (Attributes) Add a fixed delay before forwarding the request. Format: 1h/1m/1s/1ms. MUST be >=1ms. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--fault--delay--fixed_delay))
- `percentage` (Number) Percentage of requests on which the delay will be injected. If left unspecified, no request will be delayed.

<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--fault--delay--fixed_delay"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.fault.delay.fixed_delay`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--flagger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.flagger`

Required:

- `canary` (String) Name of the Canary resource that will manage the deployment.
- `namespace` (String) Namespace of the Canary resource that will manage the deployment.


<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.match`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the match condition

Optional:

- `headers` (Attributes Map) Headers to match in incoming traffic for routing forward (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--match--headers))
- `port` (Number) Port to match in incoming traffic
- `uri` (Attributes) Incoming URI to match in incoming traffic for routing forward (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--http_routes--match--uri))

<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--match--headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.match.headers`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact string match.
- `prefix` (String) Prefix-based match.
- `regex` (String) ECMAscript style regex-based match.


<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--match--uri"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.match.uri`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact string match.
- `prefix` (String) Prefix-based match.
- `regex` (String) ECMAscript style regex-based match.



<a id="nestedatt--http_routes--mirrors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `http_routes.mirrors`

Required:

- `port` (Number) The port corresponding to the service host where traffic should be routed

Optional:

- `host` (String) The host where traffic should be routed to. This should either be a FQDN or a short name for the k8s service. For example, "reviews" as destination_host will be interpreted as "reviews.ns1.cluster.local" If empty, the host will be inferred from the Service Route service field.
- `percentage` (Number) Percentage of the traffic to be mirrored. If this field is absent, the max value 100% will be mirrored.
- `subset` (String) Subset is the version of the service where traffic should be routed to



<a id="nestedatt--port_level_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `port_level_settings`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number to which traffic must be routed
- `traffic_type` (String) Type of traffic for which a route has to be generated

Optional:

- `sticky_session` (Attributes) Since we are supporting multiple types of protocols, so we expect to have separate sticky sessions for each route (i.e. for a specific port/protocol combination) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session))

<a id="nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session"></a>
### Nested Schema for `port_level_settings.sticky_session`

Optional:

- `cookie` (Attributes) Hash based on HTTP cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie))
- `header` (String) Hash based on a specific HTTP header.
- `use_source_ip` (Boolean) Hash based on the source IP address.

<a id="nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie"></a>
### Nested Schema for `port_level_settings.sticky_session.cookie`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the cookie.
- `path` (String) Path to set for the cookie.
- `ttl` (Attributes) Lifetime of the cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie--ttl))

<a id="nestedatt--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie--ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `port_level_settings.sticky_session.cookie.ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years





<a id="nestedatt--sticky_session"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sticky_session`

Optional:

- `cookie` (Attributes) Hash based on HTTP cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sticky_session--cookie))
- `header` (String) Hash based on a specific HTTP header.
- `use_source_ip` (Boolean) Hash based on the source IP address.

<a id="nestedatt--sticky_session--cookie"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sticky_session.cookie`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the cookie.
- `path` (String) Path to set for the cookie.
- `ttl` (Attributes) Lifetime of the cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sticky_session--cookie--ttl))

<a id="nestedatt--sticky_session--cookie--ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sticky_session.cookie.ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--subsets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsets`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name used to refer to the subset.

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String) Labels apply a filter over the endpoints of a service in the service registry.
- `port_level_settings` (Attributes List) Port/Protocol/StickySession combination for which routes need to be generated specifically for a subset. These settings are meant to override the global PortLevelTrafficSettings, i.e. first,  global PortLevelTrafficSettings are used to generate routes and then we use non-conflicting subset level  PortLevelTrafficSettings to modify existing routes. If provided, PortLevelTrafficSettings should be provided for  all subsets for proper load balancing. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings))
- `weight` (Number) Percentage of traffic to be sent to this subset. Weight if not specified will be assumed to be 0 if there are multiple subsets. If there is only one subset, the weight will be assumed to be 1.

<a id="nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsets.port_level_settings`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number to which traffic must be routed
- `traffic_type` (String) Type of traffic for which a route has to be generated

Optional:

- `sticky_session` (Attributes) Since we are supporting multiple types of protocols, so we expect to have separate sticky sessions for each route (i.e. for a specific port/protocol combination) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session))

<a id="nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsets.port_level_settings.sticky_session`

Optional:

- `cookie` (Attributes) Hash based on HTTP cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie))
- `header` (String) Hash based on a specific HTTP header.
- `use_source_ip` (Boolean) Hash based on the source IP address.

<a id="nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsets.port_level_settings.sticky_session.cookie`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the cookie.
- `path` (String) Path to set for the cookie.
- `ttl` (Attributes) Lifetime of the cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie--ttl))

<a id="nestedatt--subsets--port_level_settings--sticky_session--cookie--ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsets.port_level_settings.sticky_session.cookie.ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years






<a id="nestedatt--tcp_routes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tcp_routes`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of TCPRoute

Optional:

- `destination` (Attributes List) Destination host:port and subset where TCP traffic should be directed (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tcp_routes--destination))
- `match` (Attributes List) Match conditions for incoming TCP traffic (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tcp_routes--match))

<a id="nestedatt--tcp_routes--destination"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tcp_routes.destination`

Required:

- `port` (Number) The port corresponding to the service host where traffic should be routed

Optional:

- `destination_host` (String) Service host where traffic should be routed to. This should either be a FQDN or a short name for the k8s service. For example, "reviews" as destination_host will be interpreted as "reviews.ns1.cluster.local" If empty, the host will be inferred from the Service Route service field.
- `subset` (String) Subset is the version of the service where traffic should be routed to
- `weight` (Number) Weight defines the amount of traffic that needs to be routed to this specific version


<a id="nestedatt--tcp_routes--match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tcp_routes.match`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the match condition
- `port` (Number) TCP match conditions only have port in match conditions
